process_name: Indirect CP violation in K0-K0bar mixing
implementation_difficulty_reason: Existing Delta F = 2 SLL/SLR/VLL/VRR/LR1/LR2-style kaon-mixing machinery already covers epsilon_K; catalog promotion mainly needs input/provenance reconciliation.

grep_commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd warpConfig solvers tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd warpConfig solvers tests -g '!*.ipynb'
evidence: quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:211 selects key='epsilon_k' and line 212 display_name='epsilon_K'.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:735 documents epsilon_K^NP = kappa_epsilon Im(M12^NP)/(sqrt(2) Delta_m_K).
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:426 stores the modern bridge kaon B_1 constant.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:431 stores the modern bridge SM epsilon_K constant.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:1320 routes the epsilon_K system to the bridge evaluator.
tests/test_epsilon_k_physics.py:421 checks finite epsilon_K result fields.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\varepsilon_K\).  This entry covers indirect CP
violation in neutral-kaon mixing, namely the CP-violating part of the
\(K^0\)-\(\bar K^0\) \(\Delta F=2\) amplitude.  It is the headline kaon CP
constraint for anarchic warped flavor because generic new phases in
\(s\)-\(d\) mixing are tightly limited by the observed value.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical experimental value is the PDG kaon CP-violation magnitude
(\texttt{PDG2026:EpsilonK}),
\[
  |\varepsilon| = (2.228 \pm 0.011)\times 10^{-3},
\]
with the related phase \(\phi_\varepsilon=(43.5\pm0.5)^\circ\).  The local text
snapshot is
\url{flavor_catalog/references/K001/pdg2026_epsilon_k.txt}.  For the SM
comparison used by the legacy code, Brod--Gorbahn--Stamou
(\texttt{BrodGorbahnStamou:EpsilonK2020}) quote
\[
  |\varepsilon_K|_{\rm SM}
  = (2.161 \pm 0.153_{\rm param.}\pm0.076_{\rm non\mbox{-}pert.}
     \pm0.065_{\rm pert.})\times10^{-3}
  = 2.16(18)\times10^{-3}.
\]
The relevant FLAG kaon-bag input (\texttt{FLAG2024:BK}) is the \(N_f=2+1\)
average \(\hat B_K=0.7533(91)\), with
\(B_K^{\overline{\rm MS}}(2\,{\rm GeV})=0.5503(66)\).
sections.relevance:
In anarchic RS models, KK-gluon and electroweak gauge exchange generate
misaligned four-quark operators for \(K^0\)-\(\bar K^0\) mixing.  The
left-right matrix elements are chirally enhanced, and generic complex phases
make \(\varepsilon_K\) one of the strongest constraints on the KK scale.  The
CFW baseline (\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler:RSFlavor2008}) quotes KK-gluon
masses of about \(21\) TeV in standard RS and about \(33\) TeV in the composite
pseudo-Goldstone variant.
sections.post_2008:
Since the 2008 CFW baseline, the experimental central value has remained
precise.  The main updates are theory and lattice inputs.  BGS
(\texttt{BrodGorbahnStamou:EpsilonK2020}) reformulated the SM prediction with
manifest CKM unitarity and reduced the charm short-distance uncertainty,
giving the \(2.161\times10^{-3}\) central value used in the repository's
legacy \(\Delta F=2\) path.  FLAG 2024 (\texttt{FLAG2024:BK}) updates the
kaon bag averages.  The catalog should therefore keep the experimental number,
the SM theory budget, and the lattice inputs separate.
sections.validity:
As an RS constraint this is robust for generic tree-level \(\Delta F=2\)
new physics with arbitrary CP phases.  Model dependence enters through
the matching convention, QCD running, hadronic matrix elements, and the choice
of how much of \(|\varepsilon_K^{\rm exp}-\varepsilon_K^{\rm SM}|\) is assigned as
the allowed NP budget.  It is not a standalone measurement of one Wilson
coefficient; it constrains the imaginary part of the full \(K\)-mixing
amplitude.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
Coverage status: YES.  The legacy implementation registers
\texttt{epsilon\_k} in
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209}--\texttt{212}, stores the PDG and
BGS constants at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:622}--\texttt{623}, and
evaluates the observable in \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:729}.  The
QCD-running wrapper is at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:775}.  The
modern policy surface also enumerates \texttt{epsilon\_K} at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23} and routes it through
the bridge evaluator at \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:458}
and \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:1320}.  Tests exercise
the evaluator at \texttt{tests/test\_epsilon\_k\_physics.py:166} and
\texttt{tests/test\_epsilon\_k\_physics.py:421}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
Implementation difficulty: LOW.  The needed \(\Delta F=2\) operator basis,
hadronic contraction, QCD-running wrapper, and pass/fail budget already exist.
A future production cleanup should reconcile the legacy and modern frozen
input constants, but no new operator, lattice machinery, or exclusive-mode
calculation is needed for catalog-level coverage.
sections.key_references:
Process-local source keys before bibliography consolidation:
PDG2026:EpsilonK, BrodGorbahnStamou:EpsilonK2020, FLAG2024:BK, and
CsakiFalkowskiWeiler:RSFlavor2008.

anchors.anchor_string: Generic RS flavor constraints imply KK-gluon mass about 21 TeV; pseudo-Goldstone scenario about 33 TeV.

priority_rationale_short: Leading anarchic-RS kaon bound from CP-violating K mixing
priority_rationale_full:
Indirect CP violation in K0-K0bar mixing is the leading kaon-sector constraint
on anarchic-flavor RS scans. Tree-level KK-gluon exchange feeds chirally
enhanced left-right Delta S=2 operators with generic complex phases, while the
experimental value and lattice B_K inputs are tightly controlled. In the rc1.1
framing this is the kaon anchor behind the roughly 47 TeV KK-scale pressure.
